@@ -0,0 +1,95 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: de80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: Nav keys are silent no-ops in the running app — diagnose and fix the dispatch → emit → listen round-trip
+---
+## What
+
+User report: nav keys (`h/j/k/l`, arrow keys, emacs equivalents) do not cause any visible navigation in the running app. Click-to-focus works. The recent architectural overhaul (commands → Rust → navigate → focus-changed) is not observably functional, even though all 1401 UI tests and the Rust suite pass.
+
+This is a runtime regression that the existing test harness cannot catch. Tests use `setup-tauri-stub.ts` which intercepts `invoke()` at the frontend boundary — they verify that pressing `j` calls `invoke("dispatch_command", { cmd: "nav.down" })`, but they do NOT exercise:
+
+1. Rust's `dispatch_command` Tauri command routing `nav.down` through `NavigateCmd.execute`
+2. `NavigateCmd.execute` acquiring `SpatialNavigatorExt` from the command context
+3. `TauriSpatialNavigator::navigate` reading `focused_key` from the per-window `SpatialState`
+4. `SpatialState::navigate` producing a `FocusChanged` event
+5. `window.emit_to(window.label(), "focus-changed", &event)` firing on the correct window's event bus
+6. React's `useFocusChangedEffect` listener on `getCurrentWebviewWindow().listen("focus-changed", ...)` receiving the event
+7. The new moniker landing in the focused-moniker store and `data-focused` flipping on the right element
+
+At least one of steps 1–7 is silently failing in the live app. Tests don't fail because the harness short-circuits between step 1 (dispatch) and step 7 (store update) via scripted `focus-changed` responses.
+
+### Architecture snapshot (already verified by code inspection)
+
+- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-navigation/swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/nav.yaml` — declares `nav.up/down/left/right/first/last/rowStart/rowEnd` with keybindings, no client-side `execute`
+- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-navigation/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs:161-190` — registers each `nav.*` id to an `Arc<NavigateCmd(Direction::...)>`
+- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-navigation/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/nav_commands.rs:41-53` — `NavigateCmd.execute` calls `ctx.require_extension::<SpatialNavigatorExt>()` and delegates to its `navigate(window_label, direction)`
+- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-navigation/kanban-app/src/spatial.rs:518-549` — `TauriSpatialNavigator::navigate` reads `spatial_state.focused_key()` then calls `spatial_state.navigate(from_key, direction)`; on `Some(event)`, calls `window.emit_to(window.label(), "focus-changed", &event)`
+- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-navigation/kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1613-1616` — `SpatialNavigatorExt` unconditionally installed on every `CommandContext` during dispatch
+- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-navigation/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx:269-306` — `useFocusChangedEffect` listens on `getCurrentWebviewWindow()`; emit + listen labels must match
+
+### Likely failure candidates
+
+**A. Window-label mismatch.** `ctx.window_label_from_scope().unwrap_or("main")` reads the label from the dispatch's scope chain. If the frontend's `scopeChainFromScope()` doesn't include a `window:<label>` moniker, navigate gets label `"main"` — which only happens to be right if that's the actual window label. Audit: does the scope chain actually contain the window moniker? If not, `emit_to("main", ...)` might emit to a bus React isn't listening on.
+
+**B. `focused_key` is None at dispatch time.** When no click has happened yet, `spatial_state.focused_key()` returns None. `SpatialState::navigate(None, dir)` falls back to `fallback_to_first` — which is fine. But IF the active layer's first-in-layer picker returns None (empty layer, or the guard from task `01KPTVARMZR21964VRVJMP32GF` bails), `navigate` returns `None` and no event is emitted. The user's symptom is consistent with "nav fires but no event comes back."
+
+**C. `emit_to` silently fails.** `get_webview_window(window_label)` returns None if the label doesn't match an open window. No error logged, silent no-op. The `if let Some(window) = ...` path drops the emission.
+
+**D. Event listener mount/teardown race.** `useFocusChangedEffect` registers on mount via a `listen()` promise. If React re-renders or layer context churns, the listener might be replaced mid-flight. Less likely but worth a trace.
+
+### Approach — instrument, observe, fix
+
+1. **Add structured tracing** at every step in the Rust nav path. Use `tracing::info!` at:
+   - `NavigateCmd.execute` entry with `cmd_id`, `window_label`, `direction`
+   - `TauriSpatialNavigator::navigate` entry with `window_label`, `from_key`, `direction`
+   - After `spatial_state.navigate` call — log whether result was Some/None and if Some, log `prev_key` / `next_key`
+   - Inside the emit path — log whether `get_webview_window` returned Some and what label the emit used
+   - Before and after `emit_to` — confirm it completed
+2. **Add `console.warn` instrumentation** in `useFocusChangedEffect` so the JS side logs when it receives a `focus-changed` event (and what `next_key` / resolved moniker it got). Per `feedback_frontend_logging` memory, use `console.warn` for instrumentation and read via `log show`.
+3. **Run the app, press `j`, capture the macOS unified log** using `log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"' --last 2m`. Identify which step stops logging.
+4. **Fix the specific failure.** The fix is narrow — this is a diagnosis-first task, not a refactor.
+5. **Add an integration test** in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-navigation/kanban-app/tests/` (not `kanban-app/ui/src/test/`) that mounts a real Tauri `AppHandle` + `AppState`, dispatches `nav.down` through `dispatch_command`, and asserts a `focus-changed` event is emitted to the expected window label. This test must fail before the fix and pass after, and must cover the integration gap that the frontend boundary stub cannot.
+6. **Remove the instrumentation** that doesn't belong in production (keep only logs that add permanent observability value; delete raw debug breadcrumbs).
+
+### Files likely modified
+
+- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-navigation/kanban-app/src/spatial.rs` — narrow fix plus permanent tracing
+- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-navigation/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/nav_commands.rs` — tracing at command entry if diagnostic requires
+- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-navigation/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx` — `console.warn` in `useFocusChangedEffect` recovery path
+- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-navigation/kanban-app/tests/nav_dispatch_integration.rs` (new) — end-to-end integration test
+
+### Out of scope
+
+- Redesigning the dispatch pipeline
+- Adding new commands or keybindings
+- Touching the React→Rust registration path (clicks work; that side of the loop is fine)
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Pressing `h`/`j`/`k`/`l` (vim), `ArrowUp`/`Down`/`Left`/`Right` (cua), or `Ctrl+p/n/b/f` (emacs) in the running app produces a visible focus change — the focused-bar moves to a different scope
+- [ ] Pressing `G`/`End`/`Alt+>` moves focus to the last registered scope in the active layer
+- [ ] The macOS unified log shows, in order: `dispatch_command cmd=nav.<dir>`, `navigate from=<key> direction=<Direction>`, `emit focus-changed window=<label> next_key=<key>`, and on the JS side, `[focus-changed] moniker=<resolved>` — no silent step
+- [ ] Nav works both with an initial focus set (click first, then nav) AND on a fresh load with no click (fallback-to-first path)
+- [ ] New integration test in `kanban-app/tests/` fails on the current broken codebase and passes after the fix
+- [ ] All existing tests still green (`npm test` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -p kanban-app`)
+- [ ] No permanent instrumentation left in code paths where it adds noise — only logs that document behavior stay
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New: `kanban-app/tests/nav_dispatch_integration.rs` — mount a real `AppHandle` + `AppState`, register a spatial entry, dispatch `nav.down` via `commands::dispatch_command`, assert a `focus-changed` event is emitted with the expected `next_key` and on the expected window label. Must fail against HEAD, pass after the fix.
+- [ ] New: `kanban-app/tests/nav_dispatch_integration.rs` second case — `focused_key = None` at dispatch time, verify fallback-to-first fires and emits an event (covers the "no click first" path).
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p kanban-app nav_dispatch_integration` — new tests green
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -p kanban-app` — all existing tests still green
+- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — all 1401 tests still green
+- [ ] **Live verification** (per `always-verify` memory — do not skip): run `cargo tauri dev` (or the equivalent), press `h/j/k/l`, confirm focus moves. Capture `log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"' --last 2m` output showing the full round-trip.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the integration test first with the assertion for the real dispatch → emit round-trip. It will fail. The failure message + log traces locate the exact broken step.
+- Do NOT claim the bug is fixed based on unit tests or frontend tests alone — this task's definition of "fixed" is "the user sees focus move when they press `j`."
+- Per `always-verify` memory: check the macOS log yourself, do not ask the user to verify the fix. Per `frontend-logging` memory: use `console.warn` for any JS instrumentation, read the OS log via `log show`.
+- The Rust-side instrumentation stays if it adds permanent diagnostic value; the JS-side `console.warn` is temporary and gets removed once the bug is fixed.
